thesis: Speculative deep-value rebound trade — TIGR trades at 4.4x forward EPS after a -62% drawdown on the CSRC mainland-brokerage crackdown, despite mainland China contributing only ~0.6% of FY25 revenue; the June 2 earnings print will validate whether the Singapore/HK/ANZ franchise (1.25M funded accounts, $60.8B AUM, +56% revenue growth) is intact and re-rate the stock toward FUTU-parity at ~$12.65.

Set-up for a Q1 2026 earnings catalyst (May 28) with the stock trading 41% below 52W high at 23.7x forward EPS — well under the 30.4x EV-startup peer average and the $24.44 analyst PT — while three optionality drivers (first-mover China L4 robotaxi mass production, AeroHT Hong Kong IPO in 2H 2026, and Iron humanoid mass production by Q4 2026) plus the FY26 GAAP-breakeven inflection remain largely unpriced.

Mid-cycle profitability transition with 13 quarters of double-digit ad growth and a 2H26 game-pipeline catalyst stack (NCard July, San Wang Q4, Lumi Master Q4) — at 13.0x forward EPS and -52% from highs, the market prices in execution risk without paying for any pipeline wins.

At 16.8x forward P/E with 85% YoY revenue growth, 75% gross margins, and ~90% share of a $600B+ hyperscaler AI capex cycle, NVDA trades like a cyclical despite the Blackwell-to-Rubin upgrade pipeline locking in multi-year demand.

Cheapest Mag7 hyperscaler at 21.3x fwd EPS with Azure +40% YoY, $37B AI run rate (+123%), $627B commercial backlog, Copilot at 20M paid seats, and a restructured OpenAI deal through 2032 — analyst consensus PT $560.63 implies +36% upside.

Cheapest 30%-grower in Mag7 at 17.6x forward P/E with 33% revenue growth and 32.8% net margins; market is pricing zero AI-monetization optionality on top of an ad core where AI-driven ranking already lifted FB clicks +3.5% and IG conversions +3%.

Premium athleisure compounder at trough 9.9x fwd P/E with the May 27 Chip Wilson proxy overhang now resolved, a lowered June 4 earnings bar, insider buying, and September arrival of ex-Nike President Heidi O'Neill as CEO setting up a 30-50% re-rate as China +20% and India H2 2026 keep the international growth pillar intact.

Broadest international L4 autonomous franchise trading at bottom-decile 2.2x forward P/S after a +58% Q1'26 revenue beat, with Uber's $100M and 15-city commitment, $895M cash runway, and 2,000 GXR robotaxi deliveries on deck for EOY 2026.

Cheapest large-cap AI hyperscaler globally — Q4 FY26 profit collapse masks +38% Cloud growth, Qwen 3.7 frontier credibility, $19B remaining buyback, and forward P/E in 25th percentile of 5Y range, with multiple SOTP/peer-parity frameworks converging on $180-230 vs. spot ~$128.

L4 robotaxi pure-play just printed Q1 2026 revenue +145% YoY with robotaxi revenue +395%, fleet at 1,700+ and raised year-end target to 3,500 vehicles — yet stock trades 61% below 52W high with $1.4B liquidity, ~28x EV/S, and Toyota Gen-7 BOM -70% economics that make UE breakeven in 2 cities a generalizable template.

Indispensable AI bottleneck growing 35% YoY at 66.2% Q1 gross margin yet trading at 21.7x forward — below AVGO (23.1x) — with CoWoS-L sold out for 2026, NVDA pre-booked 800K+ wafers, and 5-fab N2 ramp converging four independent valuation methods to ~$500 fair value.

GM is the cheapest highly-profitable automaker — 6.0x forward earnings, 9.4% operating margin, $9-11B FY26 free cash flow, and a $6B buyback retiring ~7% of shares — with a Supreme Court tariff tailwind and FY26 EBIT guide raised to $13.5-15.5B already triggering UBS, BofA, and Deutsche Bank PT hikes.

Highly speculative photonic-quantum pure-play where Q1'26's $3.69M revenue print is ~85% bought (Luminar/NuCrypt) and gross margin is negative, but a $986M cash hoard (38% of market cap), 29.9% short float, and an expected ~$100M sovereign equity stake under the Trump $2B CHIPS quantum program create asymmetric option-like upside that should be sized as a 0.5–1.5% lottery ticket, not a core holding.

Cleanest non-NVDA AI infrastructure compounder at 23.1x fwd P/E (26% discount to AI-peer avg of 31.2x) with $73B backlog, six named custom-XPU customers, and Hock Tan guiding FY27 AI revenue 'significantly over $100B' into the June 3 print.

Cheapest multiple in TEM's public history (6.2x P/S) on a 36%-growth healthcare-AI platform pulling forward EBITDA profitability a year (FY26 guide $1.59-$1.60B / ~$65M adj EBITDA) with 27% short interest, RSI 28.7 oversold, ARK accumulating, and Q2'26 earnings Aug 7 as the re-rating catalyst.

At 26.6x fwd P/E with 22% revenue growth, 37.9% net margins, Cloud +63% YoY on a $462B backlog, and Gemini Enterprise paid MAUs +40% QoQ, GOOG is the highest-quality AI compounder among Mag7 with a 12-month base case of $347 and bull case of $533.

HBM3E sold out through 2026 and HBM4 in volume production to NVIDIA Vera Rubin have driven a 10x rally to a $1T market cap and 8.9x forward P/E on $104 forward EPS — but with peers SK Hynix (53% HBM share) and Samsung trading at 5.9x forward, MU's 18x P/S (5x its 5Y average) prices the bull case in full, leaving symmetric ~60% up / 50% down risk into the June 24 print.

Confirmed profitability inflection — Q1 2026 hit 18.8% vehicle margin and a second consecutive non-GAAP operating profit on +112% YoY revenue, yet the stock still trades at 0.14x P/S with $6.94 analyst PT (+21% upside) ahead of Q2's 110K+ delivery guide.

Contested asymmetric value at 0.39x EV/Sales and 18x fwd P/E with a $1B buyback floor, where Q1'26 deliveries (95K) beating guidance and Li i6 BEV scaling to 62% of monthly mix sets up a positive surprise into the May 28 Q1'26 print.

Hold at $871 after +649% 12-month rally: 44% revenue growth and record 47% non-GAAP gross margins are real, but 32.9x forward P/E has fully priced the AI-storage re-rating with consensus PT ($829) and HDD peer-implied fair value already below spot.

UNH is a post-DOJ-crisis turnaround at 18.4x forward EPS vs 24.4x 5Y average, with Q1 2026 MCR improvement to 83.9%, raised FY26 EPS guide to >$18.25, and CMS 2027 +2.48% MA rate finalization confirming the inflection — base case $459 (+20%), bull $597 (+55%) on Optum Health margin recovery from 3.5% to 5.1%.

AAPL at 32.4x fwd P/E — highest of Mag7 tech peers despite slowest 16.6% revenue growth — has fully priced the Gemini-powered Siri / agentic-AI WWDC-26 narrative after a +17% 30-day rally and PT hikes to $380 (BofA) / $400 (Wedbush); HOLD with $311 base / $209 bear / $420 bull.

Category-defining compounder with 92.1% renewal rate and +21.5% YoY revenue growth trading at a rich 44.5x forward EPS — Q3 FY26 print on 2026-05-28 is a real-time test of whether the premium multiple is defensible.

Inflection-point insurtech: Q1 2026 delivered +71% revenue growth, +159% gross profit, and a 16pp drop in loss ratio to 62% — the Q4 2026 adjusted EBITDA breakeven and FY27 positive-EBITDA guide are the binary catalysts that re-rate the stock from $57 toward the $70 base case (or $40 if breakeven slips).

AMD has re-rated from 'cheaper NVDA' to the primary AI accelerator diversifier — Data Center is now 57% of revenue ($5.8B in Q1'26, +57% YoY) with Meta 6GW, OpenAI, and Oracle 50K-GPU Helios commitments anchoring 2027–2029 visibility; at 38x forward EPS of $12.96 the multiple-expansion phase is done and further upside requires flawless MI400/Helios execution in H2 2026.

At $440 and 175x forward EPS, TSLA is priced as an AI/robotics platform requiring Robotaxi (3 cities now to 12 states by year-end), Optimus (production line live August 2026), and FSD (1.28M subs, +51% YoY) to all execute on aggressive timelines.

Cheapest quality semi at 21.9x forward P/E with a structural re-rating in motion as automotive (+38% YoY to $1.33B record) and a December-quarter hyperscaler custom-silicon shipment offset the fully-quantified Apple modem cliff into 2027.

At 79x forward earnings with -5.9% net margin and the highest multiple in the peer group, INTC has priced in foundry-pipeline conversion that won't show in revenue until 2027+ — peer parity ($41.95), 5y mean ($43.12) and consensus PT ($87.76) all flag 28-66% downside risk.
